paper_title: Sample Preparation and Analysis of Soil Samples from Ile de la Possession

methods.method_name: Sample Preparation for Light Microscopy (LM) and Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM)
methods.description: This method describes the preparation of soil samples for observation under light microscopy and scanning electron microscopy to analyze diatom structures.

steps.description: A small part of the raw soil material was cleaned by adding 37% H2O2 and heating to 80°C for about 1h.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean soil sample using hydrogen peroxide and heat

steps.description: The reaction was completed by addition of KMnO4.
steps.searchable_summary: Complete cleaning reaction with potassium permanganate

steps.description: Following digestion and centrifugation (three times 10 minutes at 3700 × g), cleaned material was diluted with distilled water to avoid excessive concentrations of diatom valves on the slides, and mounted in Naphrax.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge and dilute cleaned material, mount in Naphrax

steps.description: The slides were analysed using an Olympus BX53 microscope equipped with differential interference contrast (Nomarski) optics and the Olympus UC30 Imaging System.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze slides using Olympus BX53 microscope

steps.description: For SEM analyses, part of the oxidized suspension was filtered through a 5 µm Isopore™ polycarbonate membrane filter (Merck Millipore), air dried and glued to an aluminum stub.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter and prepare sample for SEM analysis

steps.description: The stub was subsequently sputter-coated with a Gold-Palladium layer of 20 nm and studied on a ZEISS Ultra SEM microscope at 3 kV.
steps.searchable_summary: Coat and analyze sample using ZEISS Ultra SEM
